title: End-to-End Testing
summary: An end to end test acts like a real user.
It clicks through the whole app in a real browser.
It is the slowest but most realistic kind.

sections.text: The most realistic test of all pretends to be a real person using your app. End to end tests open a real browser, click buttons, fill forms, and check that whole journeys work from start to finish, just as a user would experience them.

sections.title: Testing the Whole Journey
sections.text: An end to end test, often shortened to e2e, drives the full app like a user. It might open the site, log in, add an item to a cart, and check out, verifying the entire flow works together. Nothing is faked. It exercises the real app, front to back.

sections.title: The Slowest but Most Realistic
sections.text: Because it runs the whole app in a real browser, e2e testing is the slowest kind, and the most prone to being flaky if the app is slow or the network hiccups. So you write only a handful, covering the most important journeys. They give the most confidence that the app truly works for users.

media.alt: A test driving the full app through a complete user journey

sections.title: A Test Drive
sections.text: Unit tests check the engine and brakes separately. An end to end test is taking the whole car for a real drive, down the street, around the corner, into a parking spot. It is the truest test of whether the car works, even if you cannot test drive it as often as you can inspect a single part.

media.alt: A finished car taken for a full real test drive

sections.title: How an E2E Test Works
items: It opens your app in a real browser.
It acts like a user, clicking and typing.
It follows a full journey, like signing up.
It checks the journey ends correctly.
You write a few, for the most important flows.

sections.text: A common shape for a healthy test suite is many fast unit tests, fewer integration tests, and just a few end to end tests. This shape is often pictured as a pyramid.

terms.definition: A test that drives the whole app like a user.
A full flow, like signing up and checking out.
Where an e2e test runs the app.
As close to real use as testing gets.
A test that sometimes fails for unrelated reasons.

questions.text: What does an end to end test act like?
questions.explanation: An end to end test acts like a real user using the whole app.
An end to end test runs the app in a real browser.

questions.text: What kind of test is it?
questions.explanation: End to end tests are the slowest to run but the most realistic.
